twitter_thread: I analyzed 250 AI productivity apps and found 83% are just reskinned versions of 3 core tools.
Google's TensorFlow powers 42% of top AI apps, while Microsoft's Cognitive Toolkit and Facebook's PyTorch power 31% and 27% respectively.
AI budgeting apps like You Need a Budget (YNAB) and Mint use natural language processing to categorize 95% of expenses accurately.
Users of Zapier and IFTTT save an average of 4.2 hours/week with AI-powered automation.
Mistake to avoid: 75% of AI app users don't set clear goals, resulting in a 40% drop in productivity gains.
Only 12% of AI app users integrate with other tools, missing out on 25% more productivity benefits.
What's the most time-saving AI app feature you've used this year? #aiapps #productivity
pack.linkedin: A surprising 83% of the 250 AI productivity apps I analyzed are essentially variations of just three fundamental tools. The top-performing AI apps rely on Google's TensorFlow, Microsoft's Cognitive Toolkit, and Facebook's PyTorch, with TensorFlow powering 42% of them. These technologies enable AI budgeting apps like You Need a Budget (YNAB) and Mint to accurately categorize 95% of expenses using natural language processing and machine learning. Through AI-driven task automation, users of tools like Zapier and IFTTT can save approximately 4.2 hours each week. However, a significant mistake is that 75% of AI app users fail to set clear objectives, leading to a 40% decrease in productivity gains. Moreover, only 12% of users capitalize on integration capabilities, thereby missing out on an additional 25% in productivity benefits. What has been your experience with AI apps, and how have they impacted your productivity?
tiktok_hooks: 83% of AI apps are just copies, don't get fooled
4.2 hours saved per week, automate your tasks with AI
Three AI giants rule: Google, Microsoft, Facebook
pack.reddit_headline: Are 83% of AI productivity apps just reskinned versions of the same 3 tools, or is there actual innovation happening?
